title: New Places to Shop, Eat in the Miami Design District
summary: With the crowds having gone from the Dolce & Gabbana show and many from FIFA, now is a good time to make a visit to the Miami Design District, especially since new shops have opened that are brands that are probably new to you as well as restaurants. Plus, you might come across…
whatHappening: New shops and restaurants are opening in Miami's Design District as the crowds from major events like the Dolce & Gabbana show and FIFA clear out. The district is positioning itself as a destination with fresh retail and dining options that introduce lesser-known brands to visitors.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated is reporting on the Miami Design District, a premier shopping and dining destination in Miami that attracts both locals and international visitors. The district features both established luxury brands and emerging retailers.
whyMatters: The new openings provide an opportunity for consumers to discover emerging brands in a curated environment and boost foot traffic during the post-event lull. For the Design District, strategic new openings help maintain momentum and relevance in Miami's competitive retail landscape.
moreContext: The Miami Design District has historically capitalized on major events and seasonal tourism to drive visitor numbers. Following high-profile events like FIFA and designer fashion shows, the district typically experiences fluctuations in foot traffic. The introduction of new, lesser-known brands suggests a strategy to differentiate from competitors and appeal to discovery-oriented shoppers looking beyond mainstream luxury retailers. This timing also reflects broader retail trends of mixing established names with emerging boutiques to create unique shopping experiences.

title: PASTA: The Model Miami Restaurant
summary: Very reasonable prices, excellent food across the board, very good service, low noise decibel so you can easily talk, PASTA is a dream restaurant which should serve as a model to others in Miami. I'll even go so far as to say it has the best pasta in Miami. And on top of all…
whatHappening: PASTA restaurant in Miami is receiving acclaim as an exemplary dining establishment that combines excellent food quality with reasonable pricing and exceptional service. The restaurant is being highlighted as a model that other Miami establishments should emulate.
whoInvolved: PASTA is a Miami-based restaurant reviewed by Miami Curated, a local food and lifestyle publication. The establishment operates in Miami's competitive restaurant scene.
whyMatters: In a market often criticized for high prices and inconsistent quality, PASTA demonstrates that a restaurant can deliver top-tier cuisine at accessible price points while maintaining superior service standards. This model could influence how other Miami restaurants approach their pricing and operational strategies, potentially raising overall dining standards across the city.
moreContext: Miami's restaurant scene has historically struggled with balancing premium food quality against tourist-driven price inflation. PASTA distinguishes itself through multiple factors: low noise levels that enable conversation, consistently strong food offerings across the menu, and notably competitive pricing. The restaurant's pasta dishes are being recognized as potentially the best in Miami, suggesting a focused culinary philosophy that prioritizes excellence in signature dishes rather than expansive menus.

title: A Day in Sunset Harbour Miami
summary: Sunset Harbour Miami is unique. It is one of Miami Beach’s most popular, vibrant, and walkable neighborhoods. Located on the quieter bay side of South Beach, it trades the chaotic tourist energy of Ocean Drive for a chic, wellness-focused, local lifestyle. It packs a big…
whatHappening: Sunset Harbour is emerging as Miami Beach's premier alternative to the tourist-saturated Ocean Drive scene. The neighborhood is establishing itself as a vibrant, walkable destination that prioritizes wellness and local culture over party-centric tourism.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated, a local lifestyle publication, is covering the neighborhood's transformation. Sunset Harbour's appeal lies with its local residents, wellness-focused businesses, and boutique establishments that have chosen the bay-side location over beachfront alternatives.
whyMatters: This shift reflects changing travel preferences toward authentic, health-conscious experiences rather than traditional spring-break tourism. The neighborhood's rise could reshape Miami Beach's economic and cultural identity, attracting a demographic that supports local businesses and sustainable community development.
moreContext: Sunset Harbour sits on Miami Beach's bay side, offering a quieter alternative to the high-energy Ocean Drive corridor. The neighborhood has historically been overlooked in favor of beachfront areas but is now capitalizing on demand for walkable, community-oriented urban spaces. This trend aligns with broader post-pandemic travel patterns favoring wellness tourism and local experiences over mass-market attractions. The area's transformation demonstrates how secondary neighborhoods can compete with iconic destinations through lifestyle differentiation.

title: Taste the Gables is Back with 75 Restaurants
summary: The City of Coral Gables’ popular restaurant month, Taste the Gables, returns this July with its most expansive lineup to date. More than 75 restaurants, ranging from Michelin-recognized destinations to neighborhood favorites and varied cuisines, are participating, showcasing…
whatHappening: Taste the Gables, Coral Gables' signature restaurant promotion month, is returning this July with 75 participating establishments. The lineup represents the event's largest roster to date, spanning Michelin-recognized fine dining venues to casual neighborhood spots with diverse cuisines.
whoInvolved: The City of Coral Gables is organizing the event, partnering with a broad coalition of local restaurants ranging from high-end destinations to everyday eateries across multiple culinary traditions.
whyMatters: The expanded roster strengthens Coral Gables' position as a major dining destination while driving foot traffic and revenue to both marquee restaurants and smaller establishments during a traditionally slower summer month. For diners, it offers unprecedented access to varied cuisines at potentially discounted prices, making fine dining more accessible.
moreContext: Taste the Gables has established itself as an annual draw for food enthusiasts seeking to explore the city's restaurant scene. The event typically features prix-fixe menus and special pricing, making it an effective tool for restaurants to attract new customers and fill seats during summer when tourism dips. This year's 75-restaurant participation—described as the most expansive lineup yet—signals strong recovery and growth in Coral Gables' dining sector post-pandemic and demonstrates increased merchant interest in collaborative promotional events.

title: New Restaurant Reviews + More Eating News: Part 1
summary: Reviews of new restaurant openings including Casa Tua Cucina Wynwood, "Meet me at Milos "spritz and aperitivo special, a Dubai chewy cookie, new top spot for coffee lovers with a Norwegian dessert, first Cuban restaurant to get a Michelin star and a bundle for non alcoholic…
whatHappening: Miami's dining scene is experiencing a wave of new restaurant openings and culinary concepts. The additions include Casa Tua Cucina Wynwood, specialty aperitivo bars, international dessert offerings, and notably, the first Cuban restaurant to receive a Michelin star.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated is covering these developments, which involve multiple independent restaurateurs and chefs opening venues across Miami's neighborhoods, particularly in the Wynwood area. The establishments range from casual coffee shops to fine dining establishments pursuing Michelin recognition.
whyMatters: These openings signal Miami's continued evolution as a major culinary destination and reflect growing consumer interest in specialized dining experiences beyond traditional restaurant formats. The achievement of a Michelin star for a Cuban restaurant represents a significant milestone for the cuisine's recognition in fine dining circles, potentially elevating the profile of Caribbean gastronomy nationally.
moreContext: Miami has positioned itself as a foodie hub over the past decade, attracting both established and emerging culinary talent with its diverse population and affluent dining market. The Wynwood neighborhood specifically has transformed from an industrial area into a cultural and culinary destination. The emergence of aperitivo bars and specialty coffee concepts reflects broader national trends toward experiential dining and European-influenced food culture. Michelin's expansion of its restaurant guide coverage in U.S. cities beyond New York and San Francisco has increasingly validated regional culinary scenes, making Miami's achievements in this space particularly noteworthy for the local hospitality industry.
